summary: The patient has preserved linguistic understanding and normal spontaneous speech flow yet struggles with verbally reproducing information—indicating damage to the arcuate fasciculus responsible for connecting Broca’s and Wernickes’ areas. This finding reflects an isolated impairment in verbal repetition without broader aphasia features such as dysfluency or poor comprehension.
